Iraq (modern borders) — ancient Sumerian beer origin
description: Modern political border (post-1932). ORIGIN of documented beer — Ninkasi hymn, Hammurabi's Code, tavern regulations 4,000+ years ago. Modern Iraq is a minor producer; significance is cultural-historical.

Germany (modern borders) — Reinheitsgebot + lager revolution
description: Modern political border (post-1990 reunification). 1516 Reinheitsgebot purity law still informs brewing culture. Bavaria is the beer heartland — Oktoberfest, Hefeweizen, Märzen styles all originate here.

Belgium + Czech Republic (modern borders) — monastic + pilsner
description: Modern political borders. Belgium: Trappist abbey brewing tradition (lambic, abbey, saison). Czechia: Plzeň invented the pale lager style in 1842, now the world's dominant beer style.

USA (modern borders) — craft beer capital
description: Modern political border. World's largest craft brewing scene — ~9,000 breweries. German immigration built 19th-century lager industry (Milwaukee, Chicago, St. Louis). Anthony's Indianapolis craft scene is part of this.

China (modern borders) — world's #1 beer producer today
description: Modern political border. China became the world's #1 beer producer by volume in 2002 (Snow Beer is the single best-selling beer on Earth). German-founded Tsingtao (1903) was the early industrial entry.

Mexico (modern borders) — world's largest exporter by value
description: Modern political border. Mexico is the world's #1 beer EXPORTER by value (Corona, Modelo, Pacifico). Grupo Modelo (owned by AB InBev) anchors the industry.
